args.query: site:queenstownairport.co.nz wheelchair assistance arrange departure Queenstown Airport

content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Accessibility & Special Assistance\
  \ | Queenstown Airport\n- url: https://www.queenstownairport.co.nz/at-the-airport/accessible-travel/\n\
  - source: Queenstown Airport\n- domain: queenstownairport.co.nz\n\n## Content\n\n\
  # Accessibility & Special Assistance\n\nQueenstown Airport provides information\
  \ for travellers who need mobility support, accessible facilities, assistance for\
  \ non-visible disabilities, or help navigating the terminal. Travellers who expect\
  \ to need assistance are encouraged to plan before the day of travel.\n\n## Requesting\
  \ special assistance\n\nPassengers who need personal support through the terminal,\
  \ including an escort or wheelchair assistance, should contact their airline directly.\
  \ The airline is responsible for arranging this support for the passenger. Queenstown\
  \ Airport does not take the place of the airline\u2019s assistance-request process.\n\
  \nThe airport\u2019s guidance notes that wheelchair support should normally be arranged\
  \ before travel. Airline procedures may allow the request to be added during booking,\
  \ through an online account or portal, or by contacting the airline. Passengers\
  \ should follow the timing and information requirements set by their airline.\n\n\
  Wheelchair assistance from parking areas to the terminal is also arranged through\
  \ the airline rather than supplied directly as an airport booking. Travellers bringing\
  \ their own mobility equipment should notify the airline in advance so that check-in,\
  \ boarding, equipment handling, and any aircraft limitations can be addressed.\n\
  \n## Help at the airport\n\nThe Airport Information Desk can help with directions,\
  \ airport facilities, and general enquiries. It is located in the centre of the\
  \ terminal. This desk can explain where facilities are located, but personal escorts\
  \ and wheelchair support remain services to arrange through the airline.\n\n## Accessible\
  \ facilities\n\nThe terminal includes accessible toilets, gender-neutral facilities,\
  \ elevators to first-floor lounges, and designated mobility parking near the terminal.\
  \ Most of the passenger terminal is on one level. The terminal map identifies check-in,\
  \ security, arrivals, departures, accessible toilets, lifts, and assistance points.\n\
  \nAccessible taxis may be available from some local operators, and the public Orbus\
  \ service uses low-floor buses with wheelchair access. These transport services\
  \ are separate from airline-arranged assistance inside the passenger journey.\n\n\
  ## Hidden Disabilities Sunflower\n\nQueenstown Airport and its airline partners\
  \ support the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower programme. A lanyard can signal that\
  \ a traveller may need additional time, support, or understanding. A lanyard does\
  \ not itself arrange a personal escort or wheelchair. Travellers needing those services\
  \ should still contact their airline before travel.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Accessibility & Special Assistance | Queenstown Airport
url: https://www.queenstownairport.co.nz/at-the-airport/accessible-travel/

I couldn’t verify a wheelchair-assistance record for your departure because no such travel-service status is available in the linked travel records.

Queenstown Airport says:

- Wheelchair and personal assistance through the terminal must be arranged **directly with your airline**.
- Assistance should normally be requested **before travel**, either during booking, through the airline’s online account/portal, or by contacting the airline.
- Wheelchair support from the parking area to the terminal is also arranged through the airline, not booked directly with the airport.
- If you are bringing your own wheelchair or mobility equipment, notify the airline in advance so it can confirm handling and aircraft limitations.
- The airport information desk can provide directions and general help, but it does not replace the airline’s assistance-request process.

Source: [Queenstown Airport – Accessibility & Special Assistance](https://www.queenstownairport.co.nz/at-the-airport/accessible-travel/)

- Wheelchair support from the parking area to the terminal is also arranged through the airline, not booked directly with the airport.
- If you are bringing your own wheelchair or mobility equipment, notify the airline in advance so it can confirm handling and aircraft limitations.

Source: [Queenstown Airport – Accessibility & Special Assistance](https://www.queenstownairport.co.nz/at-the-airport/accessible-travel/)
